title: Prompt for Metadata Extraction
tagline: Extract tags from documents to improve RAG search filtering.
scenario: Your RAG system is returning bad results because it relies entirely on vector similarity. A search for "2024 budget" is returning the "2022 budget" because the words are similar. You need to extract metadata (Year, Department, DocType) from every document *before* embedding so you can use hard filters.
brief: Write the LLM prompt that will process raw document text and output a strict JSON block of metadata for the vector database.
deliverables: The JSON Schema required (Year, Department, DocType, Summary)
A fallback instruction (what the AI should do if the Year is not mentioned in the text)
submissionGuidance: Hybrid search (Vector Similarity + Metadata Filtering) is the industry standard for RAG. Your prompt must be robust enough to handle documents that are missing information without hallucinating dates.

rubric.label: Schema Enforcement
rubric.description: Does the prompt clearly define the JSON structure and allowed values?

rubric.label: Hallucination Prevention
rubric.description: Are there strict instructions (e.g., "return null") if data is missing?

rubric.label: Metadata Utility
rubric.description: Are the extracted fields actually useful for filtering a database?
